entries.title: An AI gateway designed to steal your data
entries.summary: Dissecting the supply chain attack on LiteLLM, a multifunctional gateway used in many AI agents. Explaining the dangers of the malicious code and how to protect yourself.

entries.title: Coruna: the framework used in Operation Triangulation
entries.summary: Kaspersky GReAT experts look into the Coruna exploit kit targeting iPhones. We discovered that the kernel exploit for CVE-2023-32434 and CVE-2023-38606 is an updated version of the Operation Triangulation exploit.

entries.title: Anatomy of a Cyber World Global Report 2026
entries.summary: The Kaspersky Security Services report describes cyberattack trends and statistics revealed by the Managed Detection and Response service. The report also includes Incident Response findings based on real-world cases identified and mitigated in 2025.

entries.title: The SOC Files: Time to “Sapecar”. Unpacking a new Horabot campaign in Mexico
entries.summary: Kaspersky SOC uncovered and analyzed a complex Horabot campaign in Mexico. In this article we share insights into how it is unleashed and how to hunt for this threat.

entries.title: Free real estate: GoPix, the banking Trojan living off your memory
entries.summary: Kaspersky GReAT experts describe the unprecedentedly complex Brazilian banking Trojan GoPix that employs memory-only implants, Proxy AutoConfig (PAC) files for man-in-the-middle attacks, and malvertising via Google Ads.

entries.title: BeatBanker: A dual‑mode Android Trojan
entries.summary: Kaspersky researchers identified a new Android Trojan dubbed BeatBanker targeting Brazil, posing as government apps and Google Play Store, and capable of both crypto mining and stealing banking data.

entries.title: Exploits and vulnerabilities in Q4 2025
entries.summary: This report provides statistical data on published vulnerabilities and exploits we researched during Q4 2025. It also includes summary data on the use of C2 frameworks in APT attacks.

entries.title: Mobile malware evolution in 2025
entries.summary: Statistics on Android malware and the most notable mobile threats of 2025: preinstalled backdoors Keenadu and Triada, spyware Trojans, the Kimwolf IoT botnet, and Mamont banking Trojans.

entries.title: Arkanix Stealer: a C++ & Python infostealer
entries.summary: Kaspersky researchers analyze a C++ and Python stealer dubbed "Arkanix Stealer", which was active for several months, targeted wide range of data, was distributed as MaaS and offered referral program to its partners.

entries.title: Divide and conquer: how the new Keenadu backdoor exposed links between major Android botnets
entries.summary: Kaspersky experts have uncovered Keenadu, a sophisticated new backdoor targeting tablet firmware as well as system-level and Google Play apps. They also revealed connections between the world's most prolific Android botnets.
